compiled_state.title: As a plugin developer, I have a triage of all existing tests classifying each as delete, replace, or keep
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Systematically triage all tests in the DSO plugin test suite, classifying each test file as: delete (pure change-detection with no behavioral value), replace (has behavioral value but uses anti-patterns), or keep (already behavioral). Focus on tests/skills/ and tests/agents/ directories first.
**Why**: Before deleting or replacing tests, we need a clear inventory so cleanup is systematic rather than ad-hoc. The pre-commit gate patterns from Epic 1f75-9b32 serve as the detection rubric.
**Scope**:
- IN: Classification of all test files in tests/skills/ and tests/agents/
- IN: Classification criteria based on pre-commit gate anti-patterns: source file grepping, existence-only assertions, string presence checks on markdown
- IN: Output as a structured list with file path, classification (delete/replace/keep), and rationale
- OUT: Actually deleting or modifying any tests (separate stories)
- OUT: Tests outside tests/skills/ and tests/agents/ (those are predominantly behavioral)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, every test file in tests/skills/ and tests/agents/ is classified as delete, replace, or keep with a rationale for each
  ← Satisfies: "Existing bloated tests are triaged"
- When this story is complete, the triage results are recorded as a ticket comment for use by subsequent cleanup stories
  ← Satisfies: "Existing bloated tests are triaged"

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Use pre-commit gate anti-patterns as detection rubric for consistent classification

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Done definition update: For each test classified, include whether it appears in .test-index and which source file(s) it maps to, so downstream stories can update .test-index correctly.
comments.author: Test

### Summary
- **Delete**: 75 files (9 Bash + 66 Python)
- **Replace**: 1 file (test_fix_bug_gate_1a.py — 1 behavioral test among 10 change-detectors)
- **Keep**: 0 files

All tests follow the same anti-pattern: read source .md files via grep/sed (Bash) or Path.read_text()/open().read() (Python) and assert on string/keyword presence. None execute scripts, call functions, or verify observable behavior.
